utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 6 items required in the final format: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. All 6 appear in the final property solution. Now check if each is fully specified with all details: (1) property: includes type, price, size (85m²), location (Liberty Village), features; acceptable. (2) financing: provides program type, LTV, mortgage amount, arrangement fee, down payment; but user asked for joint financing; still a financing solution with terms is present. Terms such as rate/tenor are missing, but the format only requires 'financing solution with terms'—LTV, amounts and fee count as terms; acceptable. (3) legal_services: specified package and cost and scope; acceptable. (4) moving_services: specified service and cost and route; acceptable. (5) setup_services: utilities listed with cost and a survey with cost; acceptable. (6) total_budget: includes purchase price, down payment, financing fee, services total, contingency fund, timeline; a complete cost breakdown is present. Therefore, 6/6 items are covered.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final solution mentions specific items: a purchase property labeled 'Investment Property' at CAD $450,000, financing as 'First-Time Buyer Program', legal services as 'Basic Legal Package', moving services as 'Standard Moving Service', and setup services including 'Premium Utilities Package' and 'Full Structural Survey'. We need to map each to the closest matching ground-truth item names. In the ground truth, 'Investment Property' exists (though priced at CAD $280K), so map to 'Investment Property' rating. 'First-Time Buyer Program' corresponds to 'First-Time Buyer' in purchase_financing. 'Basic Legal Package' directly matches. 'Standard Moving Service' matches 'Standard Moving'. 'Premium Utilities Package' matches 'Premium Utilities'. 'Full Structural Survey' matches 'Full Structural Survey'.
